outbound_strategy.reply_style: Supportive and enthusiastic — compliment specific visual elements like lighting, composition, and color palette. Keep replies concise (1-2 sentences), authentic, and community-building. Avoid generic praise.

ab_test.variant_a: 3 posts per day at slots 1-3 only
ab_test.variant_b: 4 posts per day using all 4 slots

key_insights: EN market maintains a 26x higher average engagement rate (6.1%) compared to JP (0.23%), confirming EN as the primary growth driver — continue prioritizing quality content for EN audience

Top EN performers sessypuuh (37.8% avg ER) and yuumispm (54.3% avg ER) both post at only 0.7/day with zero hashtags — the algorithm rewards high-quality visuals with minimal text over hashtag-driven discovery
Newly discovered @AndrewDark350 (209K followers) and @waifu4u2 (76K followers) are active in #AIgirl keyword space — high-value engagement targets for cross-pollination and visibility
@baharaykin (12K followers) drives 6.7% avg ER with interactive engagement-bait captions like 'you can only choose one' — testing interactive caption formats could boost reply counts
#AIbeauty keyword search shows consistent daily volume from smaller accounts, indicating growing niche awareness — early positioning with this hashtag builds long-term discoverability
risks: EN market dominated by accounts with 100K-400K followers — breaking through requires differentiated visual style and sustained engagement over weeks, not days
Top performers use zero hashtags suggesting algorithm may penalize hashtag-heavy posts — the ongoing frequency A/B test (day 2/5) should clarify optimal approach

outbound_strategy.reply_style: Polite and warm in Japanese — use casual keigo, light emoji (1-2 per reply), and specific compliments about artwork detail, color choice, or character expression. Match the peer-supportive tone of the JP AI art community.

key_insights: JP market avg engagement rate dropped slightly from 0.24% to 0.23% day-over-day, while posting frequency rose from 6.4 to 6.6/day — confirms the saturation hypothesis: more volume does not drive more engagement
Best JP performers @tororo_223 (1.68% ER at 2.0/day) and @ai_nontan_room (1.04% ER at 2.4/day) post at moderate frequency — keep JP to 3-4 quality posts rather than competing on volume
#AI美女 and #AIイラスト remain the most active JP-specific keywords with steady daily search volume — these are essential discovery tags for every JP post

JP competitors @AI_MIKENEKO (134K followers, 10 posts/day, 0.06% ER) and @hakoirikumatan (101K followers, 10 posts/day, 0.0003% ER) demonstrate that ultra-high volume dramatically depresses engagement rate — avoid exceeding 4 posts/day

Fashion category is underrepresented in JP competitor content — shifting from character (15% yesterday) to fashion (20% today) tests whether JP audience responds to style-focused content beyond the anime-illustration norm
risks: JP market has lower ceiling for engagement compared to EN — expect slower growth trajectory and set realistic milestones (focus on 0.5%+ ER before chasing follower count)
Heavy anime/illustration preference in JP market means photorealistic AI beauty content may underperform — monitor first week's ER by category and adjust content_mix accordingly
